questions.question: 中文导读：What does the adjacency matrix A[i][j] = 1 represent?
options: 中文选项：Node i has degree j
中文选项：There is an edge from node i to node j
中文选项：Node i and node j have the same label
中文选项：The shortest path from i to j has length 1
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The adjacency matrix is the core representation of a graph. A[i][j] = 1 means there is an edge connecting node i to node j. For undirected graphs, the matrix is symmetric (A[i][j] = A[j][i]).

questions.question: 中文导读：What data structure does BFS use and what does it find?
options: 中文选项：Stack; finds connected components
中文选项：Queue; finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs
中文选项：Priority queue; finds minimum spanning tree
中文选项：Hash map; finds duplicate nodes
questions.explanation: 中文解析：BFS uses a queue (FIFO) to explore all neighbors at distance k before moving to distance k+1. This guarantees that the first time a node is discovered, it is via a shortest path from the source.

questions.question: 中文导读：The graph Laplacian L = D - A of a connected graph has how many zero eigenvalues?
options: 中文选项：Zero
中文选项：Exactly one
中文选项：Equal to the number of nodes
中文选项：Equal to the number of edges
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The number of zero eigenvalues of the Laplacian equals the number of connected components. A connected graph has exactly one connected component, so exactly one zero eigenvalue. A graph with k disconnected pieces has k zero eigenvalues.

questions.question: 中文导读：In GNN message passing, what does h_v^(k+1) = sigma(W * mean({h_u^(k) : u in neighbors(v)})) compute?
options: 中文选项：The shortest path from v to all other nodes
中文选项：A new feature vector for node v by aggregating neighbor features, transforming with learned weights, and applying a nonlinearity
中文选项：The degree of node v at layer k+1
中文选项：The PageRank score of node v
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Each node collects features from its neighbors (mean aggregation), multiplies by a learned weight matrix W, and applies an activation function sigma. After k rounds, each node has information from its k-hop neighborhood.

questions.question: 中文导读：How does spectral clustering use the Fiedler vector (eigenvector of the second-smallest eigenvalue of L)?
options: 中文选项：Nodes with the largest Fiedler vector entries form one cluster
中文选项：Nodes with positive Fiedler vector values go in one group, nodes with negative values go in the other
中文选项：The Fiedler vector is used as edge weights
中文选项：The Fiedler vector determines the number of clusters
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The Fiedler vector encodes the smoothest non-trivial function on the graph. Nodes in the same tightly-connected cluster get similar values, while nodes separated by a bottleneck get values with opposite signs. The sign split partitions the graph into two clusters.
